error.signature: AI assumes a newcomer's foreign credit history counts toward a US credit score

verdict.summary: US credit scores are built only from US bureau records (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion); credit history from other countries does not transfer. Newcomers start 'credit invisible', which blocks apartments, car loans and some phone plans until a US file is established.

dead_ends.action: Apply for mainstream US credit cards or leases citing an excellent foreign credit record
dead_ends.why_fails: US lenders score from domestic bureau files; with no US history you are credit invisible and mainstream applications are declined regardless of your record abroad, and each hard inquiry can further delay approval.

dead_ends.action: Avoid all credit products to 'stay safe' during the first years
dead_ends.why_fails: A thin or empty file keeps you invisible: without tradelines reporting to the bureaus, no score forms, so future rentals, loans and even utility deposits stay expensive.

workarounds.action: Build a US file deliberately: secured card or credit-builder loan, then 6+ months of on-time payments
workarounds.how: Open a bank account, get an SSN or ITIN where eligible, start with a secured credit card, credit-builder loan, or authorized-user status, and keep utilization low. Lenders may not reject you solely for immigration status under ECOA; complaints go to the CFPB (180+ languages).
